decisions.title: Prioritize Precision Loss Warnings Across Product
decisions.context: Multiple tickets (ENG-9347, ENG-9103, ENG-7228) indicate numeric precision issues affecting data reliability. Core 'Signal>Evidence>Decision' flow breaks if users can't trust numbers. Impacts Nail the Core pillar.

arguments.for: Data trust is foundational. Precision errors undermine Decisions Captured metric. Quick wins with UI warnings.
arguments.against: Resource intensive across UI surfaces. May alarm users unnecessarily if scoped too broadly.

decisions.title: Invest in Cold Storage Read Architecture
decisions.context: ENG-9395 spike suggests need for major infrastructure work. Relates to 'Embed Deeper' by enabling long-term data access patterns. Could differentiate vs competitors (Build the Moat).
decisions.blockedBy: Spike completion and resource allocation not finalized

arguments.for: Enables retention capabilities competitors lack. Supports Ask SIFT historical queries. Unlocks enterprise use cases.
arguments.against: Large infrastructure investment. Uncertain ROI. May distract from core product gaps.

decisions.title: Resolve Keycloak Auth Stability Issues
decisions.context: ENG-8293 redirect loop and ENG-9302 FIPS requirements signal auth layer fragility. Session issues directly reduce time users spend making decisions (anti-North Star).

arguments.for: Auth issues block all product usage. High support burden. Compliance requirement (FIPS).
arguments.against: Complex infrastructure changes. Risk of breaking existing auth flows during fix.

decisions.title: Standardize UX Patterns in E2 (Explore V2)
decisions.context: DES-151 context menu audit + inconsistent behaviors (ENG-9196 spotlight, ENG-9432 user attributes) suggest UI polish gaps. Nail the Core requires consistent, intuitive UX.

arguments.for: Reduces cognitive load. Faster time-to-decision. Sets quality bar for other products.
arguments.against: Large surface area. Requires design system work. May delay feature velocity short-term.

decisions.title: Address High CPU/Performance Issues in Telemetry Rendering
decisions.context: ENG-9138 Chrome crashes on hover with high-rate data. Performance issues reduce Decisions Captured by making exploration painful. Directly impacts Canvas and Explore V2 usability.

arguments.for: Browser crashes are existential UX failures. High-rate telemetry is core use case. Quick degradation of user trust.
arguments.against: May require architectural changes to rendering pipeline. Could introduce new bugs in stable visualizations.

keyResults.name: Explore V2 Adoption (% sessions using E2)
